content: The 27th Amendment limits the ability of Congress to raise its own pay, requiring that any pay increase for lawmakers take effect only after the next election for the House of Representatives. This means that lawmakers cannot immediately benefit from their own pay raises, and must face voters before receiving increased compensation.
raw_content: The 27th Amendment limits the ability of Congress to raise its own pay, requiring that any pay increase for lawmakers take effect only after the next election for the House of Representatives.
